mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals a sharp deterioration in labor market conditions and consumer spending, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the index of industrial production reflects immediate improvements in manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term positive momentum in equity markets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns during periods when FEDFUNDS is rising, with a hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 195.0, persistent inflationary pressures force central banks to accelerate monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 195.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend levels signals underlying economic strength that improves near-term corporate earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 values show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple data release dates.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production falls below 115.0, it indicates a severe contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain constraints that weigh on corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in > 55% of observations where INDPRO is below 115.0.
